23/* sample(): private splitmix64 PRNG
24
25sample() gets its own PRNG state, completely separate from Drand01.
26That means generate_binomial(), ruif(), rbinom(), and every other caller
27of Drand01() are unaffected â€” their streams are never advanced or reseeded
28by anything sample() does.
29
30Seeding is lazy (first call) and reads from /dev/urandom; falls back to
31time()^PID on systems without it.  No aTHX needed: all calls are plain C.
32PERL_NO_GET_CONTEXT is therefore not a concern here. */
33static uint64_t sample__state  = 0;
34
35PERL_STATIC_INLINE uint64_t
36sample__mix64(void){
37        uint64_t z = (sample__state += UINT64_C(0x9e3779b97f4a7c15));
38        z = (z ^ (z >> 30)) * UINT64_C(0xbf58476d1ce4e5b9);
39        z = (z ^ (z >> 27)) * UINT64_C(0x94d049bb133111eb);
40        return z ^ (z >> 31);
41}
42

62// Uniform integer in [0, upper) â€” rejection loop, no modulo bias
63PERL_STATIC_INLINE size_t
64sample__rand(size_t upper) {
65        const uint64_t u = (uint64_t)upper;
66        const uint64_t t = (uint64_t)(-(uint64_t)u) % u;
67        uint64_t r;
68        do { r = sample__mix64(); } while (r < t);
69        return (size_t)(r % u);
70}
71// end sample() private PRNG
72
73// Ensure Perl's PRNG is seeded, matching the lazy-evaluation of Perl's rand()
74#define AUTO_SEED_PRNG() \
75        do { \
76                if (!PL_srand_called) { \
77                        (void)seedDrand01((Rand_seed_t)Perl_seed(aTHX)); \
78                        PL_srand_called = TRUE; \
79                } \
80        } while (0)

86// C helper for EXACT Non-central T-distribution CDF via Numerical Integration.
87// This perfectly replicates R's pt(..., ncp) exactness without requiring complex Beta functions.
88
458
static NV exact_pnt(NV t, NV df, NV ncp) {
89
458
50
        if (df <= 0.0) return 0.0;
90
458
        unsigned short int n_steps = 30000;
91
458
        NV step = 1.0 / n_steps;
92
458
        NV integral = 0.0, half_df = df / 2.0;
93
458
        NV log_coef = log(2.0) + half_df * log(half_df) - lgamma(half_df);
94
458
        NV root_half = 0.70710678118654752440; // 1 / sqrt(2)
95
13740000
100
        for (unsigned short i = 1; i < n_steps; i++) {
96
13739542
                NV u = i * step;
97
13739542
                NV w = u / (1.0 - u);
98                // Scaled Chi-distribution log-density
99
13739542
                NV log_M = log_coef + (df - 1.0) * log(w) - half_df * w * w;
100
13739542
                NV M = exp(log_M);
101                // Exact Normal CDF using the C standard library's erfc function
102
13739542
                NV z = t * w - ncp;
103
13739542
                NV pnorm_val = 0.5 * erfc(-z * root_half);
104
13739542
100
                NV weight = (i % 2 != 0) ? 4.0 : 2.0;
105
13739542
                integral += weight * (pnorm_val * M / ((1.0 - u) * (1.0 - u)));
106        }
107
458
        return integral * (step / 3.0);
108}

252typedef struct { const ft_support *S; NV target; NV *scratch; int mode; } ft_rc;
253/* mode 0: mnhyper(t)-target      1: mnhyper(1/t)-target
254   mode 2: pnhyper(x,t,low)-tgt   3: pnhyper(x,1/t,low)-tgt
255   mode 4: pnhyper(x,t,up)-tgt    5: pnhyper(x,1/t,up)-tgt */
256
610
static NV ft_rootf(NV t, void *ctx) {
257
610
        ft_rc *restrict r = (ft_rc *)ctx; const ft_support *restrict S = r->S;
258
610
        switch (r->mode) {
259
36
          case 0: return ft_mnhyper(S, t, r->scratch) - r->target;
260
166
          case 1: return ft_mnhyper(S, 1.0 / t, r->scratch) - r->target;
261
62
          case 2: return ft_pnhyper(S, S->x, t, 0, r->scratch) - r->target;
262
120
          case 3: return ft_pnhyper(S, S->x, 1.0 / t, 0, r->scratch) - r->target;
263
124
          case 4: return ft_pnhyper(S, S->x, t, 1, r->scratch) - r->target;
264
102
          default:return ft_pnhyper(S, S->x, 1.0 / t, 1, r->scratch) - r->target;
265        }
266}

310                   NV p = ft_pnhyper(&S, x, 1.0, 1, sc);                                     \
311                   if (p > (alpha))      { r.mode = 4; r.target = (alpha); dst = ft_zeroin(0, 1, ft_rootf, &r, FT_TOL, 1000); } \
312                   else if (p < (alpha)) { r.mode = 5; r.target = (alpha); dst = 1.0 / ft_zeroin(FT_EPS, 1, ft_rootf, &r, FT_TOL, 1000); } \
313                   else dst = 1.0; } } while (0)
314        #define FT_NCP_U(alpha, dst) do {                                                    \
315          if (x == hi) { dst = INFINITY; } else {                                          \
316                   NV p = ft_pnhyper(&S, x, 1.0, 0, sc);                                     \
317                   if (p < (alpha))      { r.mode = 2; r.target = (alpha); dst = ft_zeroin(0, 1, ft_rootf, &r, FT_TOL, 1000); } \
318                   else if (p > (alpha)) { r.mode = 3; r.target = (alpha); dst = 1.0 / ft_zeroin(FT_EPS, 1, ft_rootf, &r, FT_TOL, 1000); } \
319                   else dst = 1.0; } } while (0)

338/*Helpers for lm Linear Regression: OLS Matrix Math & Formula Parsing
339 * -----------------------------------------------------------------------
340 Sweep operator for symmetric positive-definite matrices (e.g., XtX).
341 This gracefully handles collinearity by bypassing aliased columns.
342 Utilizes a relative tolerance check to prevent dropping micro-variance features.*/

358
static int sweep_matrix_ols(NV *restrict A, size_t n, bool *restrict aliased) {

358

350        }

352                // Check pivot for collinearity using a RELATIVE tolerance
353                // (Fallback to a tiny absolute tolerance of 1e-24 to catch literal zero vectors)
354

571/* Kendall's tau-b between two n-element arrays.
572
573 *   tau-b = (C âˆ’ D) / sqrt((C + D + T_x)(C + D + T_y))
574 *
575 * where C = concordant pairs, D = discordant, T_x = pairs tied only on
576 * x, T_y = pairs tied only on y.  Joint ties (both zero) are excluded
577 * from numerator and denominator, matching R's cor(method="kendall").
578 * Returns NAN when the denominator is zero.                             */
579

779/* -----------------------------------------------------------------------
780 * Exact Spearman p-value via exhaustive permutation enumeration.
781 *
782 * Under H0, all n! orderings of ranks are equally probable.  We visit
783 * every permutation of {1..n} with Heap's algorithm (O(n!), no allocs
784 * inside the loop) and count how many yield S â‰¤ s_obs ("lower tail",
785 * i.e. rho â‰¥ rho_obs) and how many yield S â‰¥ s_obs ("upper tail").
786 *
787 * Mirrors R's default: exact = (n < 10) with no ties.
788 * Valid up to n = 9 (362 880 iterations â€” negligible cost).
789 * ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */
790
2
static NV spearman_exact_pvalue(NV s_obs, size_t n, const char *restrict alt) {
791
2
        int *restrict perm = (int*)safemalloc(n * sizeof(int));
792
2
        int *restrict c    = (int*)safemalloc(n * sizeof(int));
793
12
100
        for (size_t i = 0; i < n; i++) { perm[i] = i + 1; c[i] = 0; }
794
795
2
        long count_le = 0, count_ge = 0, total = 0;
796
797        #define TALLY_PERM() do {                                    \
798          NV s_ = 0.0;                                     \
799          for (int ii = 0; ii < n; ii++) {                    \
800                   NV d_ = (NV)(ii + 1) - (NV)perm[ii];\
801                   s_ += d_ * d_;                                   \
802          }                                                    \
803          if (s_ <= s_obs + 1e-9) count_le++;                 \
804          if (s_ >= s_obs - 1e-9) count_ge++;                 \
805          total++;                                             \
806        } while (0)
807
808
12
100
50
50
        TALLY_PERM();   /* initial permutation [1, 2, ..., n] */
809
810
2
        unsigned int k = 1;
811
412
100
        while (k < n) {
812
410
100
                if (c[k] < k) {
813                        int tmp;
814
238
100
                        if (k % 2 == 0) {
815
88
                                 tmp = perm[0]; perm[0] = perm[k]; perm[k] = tmp;
816                        } else {
817
150
                                 tmp = perm[c[k]]; perm[c[k]] = perm[k]; perm[k] = tmp;
818                        }
819
1428
100
100
100

820
238
                        c[k]++;
821
238
                        k = 1;
822                } else {
823
172
                        c[k] = 0;
824
172
                        k++;
825                }
826        }
827        #undef TALLY_PERM
828
2
        Safefree(perm); Safefree(c);
829        /* p_le = P(S â‰¤ s_obs) â‰¡ P(rho â‰¥ rho_obs)  â€” upper rho tail
830        * p_ge = P(S â‰¥ s_obs) â‰¡ P(rho â‰¤ rho_obs)  â€” lower rho tail  */
831
2
        NV p_le = (NV)count_le / (NV)total;
832
2
        NV p_ge = (NV)count_ge / (NV)total;
833
834
2
50
        if (strcmp(alt, "greater") == 0) return p_le;
835
2
50
        if (strcmp(alt, "less")    == 0) return p_ge;
836        /* two.sided: 2 Ã— the smaller tail, clamped to 1 */
837
2
50
        NV p = 2.0 * (p_le < p_ge ? p_le : p_ge);
838
2
50
        return (p > 1.0) ? 1.0 : p;
839}

841 * Exact Kendall p-value via Mahonian Numbers (Inversions distribution)
842 * Matches R's behavior for N < 50 without ties.
843 * ----------------------------------------------------------------------- */
844
4
static NV kendall_exact_pvalue(size_t n, NV s_obs, const char *restrict alt) {
845
4
        long max_inv = (long)n * (n - 1) / 2;
846
4
        NV *restrict dp = (NV*)safemalloc((max_inv + 1) * sizeof(NV));
847
48
100
        for (long i = 0; i <= max_inv; i++) dp[i] = 0.0;
848
4
        dp[0] = 1.0;
849        /* Build the distribution of inversions via DP */
850
20
100
        for (size_t i = 2; i <= n; i++) {
851
16
                NV *restrict next_dp = (NV*)safemalloc((max_inv + 1) * sizeof(NV));
852
192
100
                for (long k = 0; k <= max_inv; k++) next_dp[k] = 0.0;
853
16

100

100

857

                                 sum += dp[k - j];
858                        }

860

861                }
862
16
                Safefree(dp);
863
16
                dp = next_dp;
864        }
865        // Convert S statistic to target number of inversions
866
4
        long i_obs = (long)round((max_inv - s_obs) / 2.0);
867
4
50

868
4
50
        if (i_obs > max_inv) i_obs = max_inv;
869
4
        NV p_le = 0.0; /* P(S <= S_obs) */
870
40
100
        for (long k = i_obs; k <= max_inv; k++) p_le += dp[k];
871
4
        NV p_ge = 0.0; /* P(S >= S_obs) */
872
16
100
        for (long k = 0; k <= i_obs; k++) p_ge += dp[k];
873
4
        Safefree(dp);
874
4
50
        if (strcmp(alt, "greater") == 0) return p_ge;
875
4
100
        if (strcmp(alt, "less") == 0) return p_le;
876        // two.sided
877
2
50
        NV p = 2.0 * (p_ge < p_le ? p_ge : p_le);
878
2
50
        return p > 1.0 ? 1.0 : p;
879}

624

955/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
956 * print_string_row: emit one record.
957 *
958 * Quoting contract (matches the behaviors your tests pin down):
959 *      - A field is quoted IFF it contains the separator string, a double
960 *        quote, or a newline / carriage return. Quoting is per-field, so in a
961 *        TSV "hello,world" stays bare while "tab\tin" becomes "tab    in".
962 *      - Inside a quoted field, embedded double quotes are doubled:
963 *        p"q -> "p""q"   (RFC 4180 style)
964 *      - A NULL or zero-length field prints as NOTHING between separators:
965 *        a,,c  -- never '' or "". A zero-length field cannot contain a
966 *        separator, quote, or newline, so it never needs quoting.
967 *      - The separator is treated as a string (strstr), so multi-character
968 *        separators work; an empty separator never triggers quoting.
969 *
970 * Returns nothing; I/O errors surface on PerlIO_close at the call site.
971*/
972
326
static void print_string_row(pTHX_ PerlIO *restrict fh,
973        const char **restrict fields, size_t n, const char *restrict sep)

1089/* Exact CDF for Wilcoxon Signed Rank: P(V <= q)

12

50

1369        // Bisect to find the root
1370

1379
502
100
                if (high - low < 1e-12) break;
1380        }
1381
12
        return (low + high) / 2.0;
1382}

1392        NV  ms_within;   /* ss_within  / denom_df         */

1394        IV      n;           /* total observations            */
1395        bool     var_equal;   /* 0 = Welch, 1 = classic        */

12
50
                res.ms_within  = (res.denom_df > 0.0) ? sswg / res.denom_df : 0.0;
1484
12
                Safefree(w_i);
1485        }
1486        // upper-tail p-value  P(F â‰¥ statistic)
1487
12
        res.p_value = 1 - pf(res.statistic, res.num_df, res.denom_df);
1488
12
        Safefree(n_i);    Safefree(m_i);    Safefree(v_i);
1489
12
        return res;
1490}

1492/* â”€â”€ parse_formula
1493 *
1494 *  Splits "response ~ factor" into two NUL-terminated, heap-allocated
1495 *  strings.  Leading/trailing whitespace is stripped from each side.
1496 *  Returns 1 on success, 0 on failure (malformed / missing '~').
1497 *  Caller must Safefree() both *lhs and *rhs on success. */
1498static int
1499
8
parse_formula(const char *formula, char **lhs, char **rhs)
1500{
1501
8
        const char *restrict tilde = strchr(formula, '~');
1502
8
100
        if (!tilde) return 0;
1503
1504        // left-hand side: trim trailing whitespace
1505
6
        const char *restrict l_start = formula;
1506
6
        const char *restrict l_end   = tilde - 1;
1507
12
50
100
        while (l_end >= l_start && isspace((unsigned char)*l_end)) l_end--;
1508
6
50
        if (l_end < l_start) return 0; /* empty LHS */
1509
1510        // right-hand side: trim leading whitespace */
1511
6
        const char *restrict r_start = tilde + 1;
1512
12
50
100
        while (*r_start && isspace((unsigned char)*r_start)) r_start++;
1513
6
        const char *restrict r_end = r_start + strlen(r_start) - 1;
1514
6
50
50
        while (r_end >= r_start && isspace((unsigned char)*r_end)) r_end--;
1515
6
50
        if (r_end < r_start) return 0; /* empty RHS */
1516
1517
6
        size_t llen = (size_t)(l_end - l_start + 1);
1518
6
        size_t rlen = (size_t)(r_end - r_start + 1);
1519
1520
6
        *lhs = (char *)safemalloc(llen + 1);
1521
6
        *rhs = (char *)safemalloc(rlen + 1);
1522
6
        memcpy(*lhs, l_start, llen); (*lhs)[llen] = '\0';
1523
6
        memcpy(*rhs, r_start, rlen); (*rhs)[rlen] = '\0';
1524
6
        return 1;
1525}
1526
1527/* â”€â”€ build_groups_from_formula â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
1528 *
1529 *  Takes parallel response[] and label[] arrays (each length n) and
1530 *  partitions them into groups, filling:
1531 *    out_flat[]  â€“ observations sorted into contiguous group blocks
1532 *    out_sizes[] â€“ number of observations per group  (caller allocates n
1533 *                  slots for both; actual group count returned via *out_k)
1534 *    out_names   â€“ if non-NULL, receives a heap-allocated char** of k
1535 *                  group-name strings (caller must free each and the array)
1536 *
1537 *  Group identity is the string representation of each label element
1538 *  (SvPV_nolen), so integer 0 and string "0" are the same group.
1539 *  Groups are ordered by first appearance in label[], matching R's
1540 *  factor level ordering from stack().
1541 *
1542 *  Returns 1 on success; 0 if any validation error (sets errbuf).
1543 */
1544#define OWT_MAX_GROUPS 1024   /* sane ceiling; ANOVA with >1024 groups is absurd */
1545
1546
4

1658/* c_dnorm: Normal distribution PDF
1659 *
1660 * Mathematically identical to R's dnorm4.
1661 * Includes Morten Welinder's precision improvements for extreme tails.
1662*/
1663
54
static NV c_dnorm(NV x, NV mu, NV sigma, int give_log) {
1664        // Propagate NaNs
1665
54
50
50
50
        if (isnan(x) || isnan(mu) || isnan(sigma)) return x + mu + sigma;
1666
54
50
        if (sigma < 0.0) {
1667
0
          warn("dnorm: standard deviation must be non-negative");
1668
0
          return NAN;
1669        }
1670
54
50
        if (isinf(sigma)) return 0.0;
1671
54
50
50
0
        if ((isnan(x) || isinf(x)) && mu == x) return NAN; // x-mu is NaN
1672        // Dirac delta behavior for zero variance
1673
54
50
0
        if (sigma == 0.0) return (x == mu) ? INFINITY : 0.0;
1674
1675        // Standardize x
1676
54
        x = (x - mu) / sigma;
1677
54
50
50
        if (isnan(x) || isinf(x)) return 0.0;
1678
54
        x = fabs(x);
1679        // Catch massive limits early to prevent math overflow
1680
54
50
        if (x >= 2.0 * sqrt(DBL_MAX)) return 0.0;
1681
54
100
        if (give_log) {
1682
2
                return -(M_LN_SQRT_2PI + 0.5 * x * x + log(sigma));
1683        }
1684        // Naive formula for standard bodies
1685
52
100
        if (x < 5.0) {
1686
48
          return M_1_SQRT_2PI * exp(-0.5 * x * x) / sigma;
1687        }
1688        // Underflow boundary check using IEEE float characteristics
1689
4
50
        if (x > sqrt(-2.0 * M_LN2 * (DBL_MIN_EXP + 1.0 - DBL_MANT_DIG))) {
1690
0
          return 0.0;
1691        }
1692        /* Splitting x to dodge floating point inaccuracies in x^2 for large x.
1693        * x = x1 + x2, where |x2| <= 2^-16
1694        * trunc() safely substitutes R_forceint() */
1695
4
        NV x1 = ldexp(trunc(ldexp(x, 16)), -16);
1696
4
        NV x2 = x - x1;
1697
4
        return (M_1_SQRT_2PI / sigma) * (exp(-0.5 * x1 * x1) * exp((-0.5 * x2 - x1) * x2));
1698}

1826//
1827// filter() helpers â€” place this block in the C section, ABOVE the MODULE line

1918/* Finish the current record: push the pending field, hand the row to the
1919 * callback (streaming) or to @$data (slurp), and start a fresh row.
1920 *
1921 * Ownership: the row AV's single reference is transferred to a MORTAL RV
1922 * (newRV_noinc + sv_2mortal). On the normal path the inner FREETMPS releases
1923 * it; if the callback dies, the unwind's FREETMPS releases it just the same.
1924 * If the callback kept a copy of the ref, that copy bumped the refcount and
1925 * the row survives for the caller -- exactly the old semantics, minus the
1926 * leak and minus one SvREFCNT_dec per row. */

1973/* Sort by a named column: pre-fetched cell SVs plus a numeric/string flag. */

1984        SV  *b_sv;              /* scalar currently aliased to package $b */

2180SV *aoh2hoa(data)
2181        SV *data
2182        CODE:
2183        {
2184/*
2185 * aoh2hoa($aoh) -- transpose an Array-of-Hashes into a
2186 * Hash-of-Arrays.
2187 *
2188 *   in : arrayref of hashrefs (rows)  [ {a=>1,b=>2}, {a=>3} ]
2189 *   out: hashref of arrayrefs (cols)  { a=>[1,3], b=>[2,undef] }
2190 *
2191 * - Columns are the union of all row keys.
2192 * - Every column has exactly scalar(@$aoh) elements; cells absent
2193 *   from a given row are undef (kept as cheap holes, not SVs).
2194 * - Values are copied, so the result is independent of the input
2195 *   (a value that is itself a reference is copied shallowly, just
2196 *   like Perl's  $col->[$i] = $row->{$k} ).
2197 * - A row that is not a hashref contributes undef to every column
2198 *   at its index (skipped, not fatal).
2199*/
2200                AV *restrict aoh;
2201                HV *restrict out;
2202                SSize_t n, i;
2203                HE *restrict he;

3108
3109SV *col2col(data, cmd, cols = &PL_sv_undef, ...)
3110                SV *data
3111                SV *cmd
3112                SV *cols
3113        CODE:
3114        {
3115// Only these cross the section boundaries (build -> loop -> cleanup);
3116// everything else is declared at its point of use just below.
3117
106
                SV *restrict cv_sv = NULL;
3118
106
                size_t ncols = 0, nrows = 0;
3119
106
                AV *restrict names_av = newAV();
3120
106
                NV **restrict col_val = NULL;
3121
106
                char **restrict col_def = NULL;
3122
106
                short int na_mode = 0;  // 0 = pairwise, 1 = omit, 2 = keep; see section 0
3123
106
                bool skip_errors = TRUE;        // skip.errors (default true): trap a croaking block, store its message
3124// 0. options. They may be given either as trailing name => value pairs
3125//    (after the positional cols), or - so no placeholder is needed when
3126//    there is no column restriction - as a single hash ref in cols's
3127//    place, e.g. col2col($data, 'cor', { 'skip.errors' => 1 }).
3128//    `na` controls how undef is handled when one column is paired with
3129//    another:
3130//      'pairwise' (default) - a row counts for the (a,b) pair only if
3131//          BOTH columns are defined there, so the block gets two equal
3132//          length, aligned columns. This is what paired stats (cor) want.
3133//      'omit'   - each column independently drops its own undef values,
3134//          so the two columns may differ in length. This is what unpaired
3135//          tests (t_test, kruskal_test) want: a gap in one column must not
3136//          throw away a good value in the other.
3137//      'keep'   - every row passes through and undef reaches the block.
3138//    rm.undef / rm.na (bool) remain as aliases: true => 'pairwise' (the
3139//    old default), false => 'keep'.
3140//    skip.errors (bool, default true): a block that croaks for a pair
3141//    does not abort col2col; instead the first line of its error message
3142//    is stored as that cell's value, so the result shows which
3143//    (outer => inner) pair failed and why. Set it false to make a croak
3144//    propagate and abort the whole call instead.
3145
106
                SV *restrict cols_eff = cols;
3146
106
                bool na_set = FALSE, rm_set = FALSE;
3147#define C2C_DECODE_OPT(ONAME, OL, OVAL) do { \
3148                if ((OL) == 2 && memEQ((ONAME), "na", 2)) { \
3149                        STRLEN vl_; const char *restrict nv_ = SvPV((OVAL), vl_); \
3150                        if (vl_ == 8 && memEQ(nv_, "pairwise", 8)) na_mode = 0; \
3151                        else if (vl_ == 4 && memEQ(nv_, "omit", 4)) na_mode = 1; \
3152                        else if (vl_ == 4 && memEQ(nv_, "keep", 4)) na_mode = 2; \
3153                        else croak("col2col: na must be 'pairwise', 'omit' or 'keep'"); \
3154                        na_set = TRUE; \
3155                } else if (((OL) == 8 && memEQ((ONAME), "rm.undef", 8)) || ((OL) == 5 && memEQ((ONAME), "rm.na", 5))) { \

8

8
100

12
100

60
100

50

5111
50

5112
50

5113                                  }
5114                                }
5115                        } else {
5116
6

5117
6
100
                                if (strcmp(method, "spearman") == 0) {

5119
2

5120
2

5122
2

5124
12
100
                                  for (size_t i = 0; i < n; i++) {
5125
10

5126
10

5127
10
                                                NV dy = ry[i] - mean_y;
5128
10
                                                mean_y += dy / (i + 1);
5129
10

5130                                  }
5131
2

5134

100

5135
20

5138

5139

5140                                  }
5141                                }
5142
5143                                // Unbiased Sample Covariance (N - 1) for Pearson & Spearman
5144
6

5145                        }
5146
8

2

6268                                 */
6269

6270                        }

6273
2

6275
2

6276

6277
2
                        NV sigma  = exp(sig_val);
6278
2

6279
2

9325
6

6

9327
6

9328        }
